Job Description:
Our Company

Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen.

We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours!

The Opportunity
Do you love to solve complex business problems and use data to find the signal in the noise? Join the Product and Customer Analytics team at Adobe! We are a high-impact, high-visibility team well-suited to individuals with a highly quantitative, inquisitive bent of mind. We use data science to quantify and optimize the product experience for our customers! The ideal candidate will be able to work autonomously and will have a technical background with great communication and project management skills.

You will use data to help product, marketing and engineering teams achieve operational and execution efficiencies. Your success is predicated on finding opportunities and providing insights that enable our enterprise business to lower customer churn, increase user engagement, and facilitate product-led growth.
We are looking for a Data Scientist to improve the adoption and utilization of all our Experience Cloud products. The opportunity is huge, ranging from crafting experiments, building machine learning models to developing new product features. We need a creative self-starter who is passionate about data and knowledge of using data science to enhance customer experience.

What You’ll Do
As a data scientist in a multi-functional group of product managers, marketers, and software developers, you will tap into the underlying usage data, and generate insights to develop highly effective programs for Adobe’s Experience Cloud products. You will analyze changes in user engagement which are key drivers of customer growth and retention.
You will also work with data engineers to automate data pipelines to scale user analytics. You will establish relationships with partners across the Experience Cloud business unit to execute team’s projects.
What You Need to Succeed
Strong Proficiency with statistical programming software like Python (pandas, scikit-learn) and/or R (dplyr, ggplot2)
Experience with predictive modeling and machine learning algorithms (supervised and unsupervised)
Strong knowledge of Statistics & Hypothesis Testing
Experience in querying, manipulating, and analyzing large data sets using SQL and/or SQL-like languages.
Experience with version control software GitHub and data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
Understanding of experimental design & measurement (A/B Testing)
Communicate effectively and manage relationships with partners coming from both technical and non-technical backgrounds
Develop relationships with engineers and stakeholders

The Role
The Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (DSAI) Co-op Program is a 6-month experience designed for students studying towards a Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD degree in applying machine learning methods to problems in clinical trial recruitment and regulatory affairs,. As part of our mission to “Build The Digital Biotech Company” we see a central role for DSAI in accelerating the science and engineering at the heart of our innovative culture. Clinical trial Recruitment - Leveraging historical search data and patient referrals proximity to a site to identify the likelihood of a patient to enroll in a clinical trail

Current B.S., M.S., or Ph.D. students in Computer Science, Applied Math, Engineering or other related quantitative STEM disciplines.

Solid understanding of probabilistic models and applied statistics.

Fluency in Python, especially the scientific Python stack (Jupyter/Pandas/Scikit-learn/PyTorch).

Work experience in Unix/Linux environment.

Prior experience with deep learning or machine learning projects.

Excellent communication skills (verbal, written, and virtual/remote).

Willing and able to relocate to Cambridge, MA for the co-op.

Indefinite right to work in the United States without the need for sponsorship now or in the future.

Here’s What You’ll Bring to the Table (Preferred Qualifications)

Familiarity with both the theory and practice of Natural Language Processing/Understanding (SpaCy/NLTK/transformers/word embeddings/etc.).

Experience in probability modeling of a real-world system involving the inherent uncertainty

Familiarity with large language models and its applications

Experience in finetuning large language models on custom infrastructure

Experience in identifying, procuring, and integrating external data sources.

Familiarity with software development processes and git version control.

Deliver documented and unit-tested code, visualizations, in-depth user-facing documentation, and stakeholder-facing written and oral presentations on the chosen project.

A desire to make an impact as part of a high-growth, transformational company that is Bold, Relentless, Curious, and Collaborative.

Company Description
Square builds common business tools in unconventional ways so more people can start, run, and grow their businesses. When Square started, it was difficult and expensive (or just plain impossible) for some businesses to take credit cards. Since we opened our doors in 2009, the world of commerce has evolved immensely, and so has Square. After enabling anyone to take payments and never miss a sale, we saw sellers stymied by disparate, outmoded products and tools that wouldn’t work together.
So we expanded into software and started building integrated, omnichannel solutions – to help sellers sell online, manage inventory, offer buy now, pay later functionality through Afterpay, book appointments, engage loyal buyers, and hire and pay staff. Across it all, we’ve embedded financial services tools at the point of sale, so merchants can access a business loan and manage their cash flow in one place. Afterpay furthers our goal to provide omnichannel tools that unlock meaningful value and growth, enabling sellers to capture the next-generation shopper, increase order sizes, and compete at a larger scale.

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering any, Technology or related and 2 yrs of exp to analyze large amounts of information to discover trends and patterns. Overview:
Arcfield is a leading provider of full lifecycle, mission-focused systems engineering and integration capabilities to the U.S. government and its allies. The company has more than 60 years of proven experience providing advanced engineering and analysis, IT and C5ISR capabilities to support our nation’s most critical national security missions. Headquartered in Chantilly, VA and with 16 offices around the world, Arcfield employs approximately 1,200 engineers, analysts, IT specialists, and other professionals who put our customers’ missions first, helping them solve their most complex challenges through innovations in modeling, simulation and analysis, digital transformation and C5ISR. Visit arcfield.com for more details.
Responsibilities:
The Data Scientist will support the Sponsor's organization responsible for championing governance and quality. The organization is focused on improving efficiency and capabilities to build, deliver, and sustain IT Network solutions. The selected candidate will be responsible for developing the means for measuring and visually reporting real-time, or near real-time, data for quality assurance Service Level Agreements (SLA) and Service Level Objectives (SLO). This will consist of working with multiple stakeholders in various groups to define, develop, and institutionalize strategic processes across multi-fabric information environments.
Qualifications:
EEO Statement:

Overview:
The Data Scientists will take a lead role in the end-to-end process of data science initiatives with limited supervision, including but not limited to model design, structure, development and post-deployment model performance monitoring. By conducting innovative research through advanced analytics and technical sophistication, the Data Scientist will contribute to create the economic value at scale from both future growth and profitability perspectives.

In this role, you will be set on a constant “treasure hunt” for new data sources to apply the latest techniques for modeling projects. You will have unparalleled opportunity to apply state-of-the-art machine learning techniques to real world datasets. You will work on a team of PhD level statisticians to create ground-breaking predictive models using new analytical techniques that determine insurance pure premiums, customer life time value, customer buying behaviors and other related topics. This work will be used in setting pricing, underwriting, marketing, policy and claims service, fraud detection and product design strategies for the Plymouth Rock group of Companies. The role is not simply research: you will be part of a tactical team driving the profit and customer growth of an assigned business segment. The analysis and modeling will be tested via implementation both broadly and on a pilot basis to allow us to learn and refine our approaches in rapid succession.

About InterDigital
InterDigital develops fundamental wireless and video technologies that are at the core of mobile devices, networks, and services worldwide. We solve many of the industry's most critical and complex technical challenges, inventing solutions for more efficient broadband networks and richer multimedia experiences, years ahead of market deployment. InterDigital has licenses and strategic relationships with many of the world's leading wireless companies. Founded in 1972, InterDigital is listed on NASDAQ and is included in the S&P MidCap 400® index. For more information please visit www.interdigital.com.
Summary
InterDigital is currently seeking a Data Scientist to work in our IS Department. We are looking for a Data Scientist to provide insights into the business through statistical analysis, data mining and data visualization techniques. The successful candidate will be intricately involved with our Data Management and Information Delivery team in the governance, development and maintenance of our reporting solution including the refinement of a data framework and implementation of Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence solutions.

MOTOR Information Systems, an owned subsidiary of Hearst, is actively seeking a Data Scientist who possesses talent, passion, and expertise in extracting valuable knowledge from complex datasets, contributing to the development of data-driven strategies, and improving the overall efficiency of our operations. MOTOR’s Insights team is a new team with an exponential growth opportunity – both in terms of technology and personnel, and we are looking for someone to share our mission that will transform our company’s future. As a Data Scientist, we expect this role to be instrumental in solving complex business problems, providing actionable insights, and supporting evidence-based decision-making. This position offers excellent career growth and promotional opportunities, stellar compensation, and an opportunity to work with the world's premier provider of aftermarket automotive data. Hearst and MOTOR Information Systems could very well be your best and last position in a rewarding and fulfilling career!

Some 18,000 financial services and healthcare organizations, from the world's largest institutions to local firms, manage and account for their investments using SS&C's products and services.
Job Description
Senior Data Scientist
Location: Waltham, MA | Hybrid
Get To Know The Team:
Your deep experience with artificial intelligence, NLP, language models and advanced analytics is matched by a passion to build great products, lead innovation, be a mentor and guide to other Engineering team members. In the past you have been part of a startup or corporate innovation team working in fast-moving environments. You can point to numerous examples which have demonstrated your ability to creativity solve technical challenges.

Job Description:

Ignite is an ISO 9001:2015 and CMMI Services Level 3 certified, Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB), headquartered in Huntsville, AL. By design, Ignite is a provider of professional services to customers in educational, federal, and commercial industries and in every action seeks to be the preeminent provider within this business space. Ignite upholds our values of competency, collaboration, innovation, reliability, and results through everything we do.
Ignite is currently seeking a driven, detail-oriented Data Scientist to join our team supporting our government partner. This position is expected to be onsite within a government facility. This applicant is someone who creatively approaches a variety of requirements to meet intelligence needs, can balance work on multiple tasks, and will work collaboratively with other scientists, engineers, and analysts.

Passionate about helping clients make the most of their analytics investments? Driven to succeed in a competitive and rapidly-changing business? Looking for an opportunity with a premiere provider of specialized consulting services with a collaborative, team oriented approach?
We are currently accepting applications from talented individuals who seek to advance their career within a fast-paced and challenging environment. We offer all CognoWiz team members a highly competitive compensation package that includes benefits and opportunities for both personal and professional growth.
Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering any, Technology or related and 2 yrs of exp to analyze large amounts of information to discover trends and patterns. Manipulate, transform and combine datafrom multiple data sources for use in analytics and reporting. Produce high ad hoc and standard reports, using R or SAS procedures. Efficiently identify and correct syntax and programming logic errors in SQL, Tableau and SAS code. Integrate R/SAS analysis with Tableau to deliver the results of statistical analysis within Tableau dashboards. Perform inferential statistical analysis in R or SAS, including statistical tests for significance, such as null-hypothesis testing, to validate results of analysis.

Overview:
Arcfield is a leading provider of full lifecycle, mission-focused systems engineering and integration capabilities to the U.S. government and its allies. The company has more than 60 years of proven experience providing advanced engineering and analysis, IT and C5ISR capabilities to support our nation’s most critical national security missions. Headquartered in Chantilly, VA and with 16 offices around the world, Arcfield employs approximately 1,200 engineers, analysts, IT specialists, and other professionals who put our customers’ missions first, helping them solve their most complex challenges through innovations in modeling, simulation and analysis, digital transformation and C5ISR. Visit arcfield.com for more details.
Responsibilities:
The Data Scientist will support the Sponsor's organization responsible for championing governance and quality. The organization is focused on improving efficiency and capabilities to build, deliver, and sustain IT Network solutions. The selected candidate will be responsible for developing the means for measuring and visually reporting real-time, or near real-time, data for quality assurance Service Level Agreements (SLA) and Service Level Objectives (SLO). This will consist of working with multiple stakeholders in various groups to define, develop, and institutionalize strategic processes across multi-fabric information environments.
Qualifications:
EEO Statement:

Overview:
The Data Scientists will take a lead role in the end-to-end process of data science initiatives with limited supervision, including but not limited to model design, structure, development and post-deployment model performance monitoring. By conducting innovative research through advanced analytics and technical sophistication, the Data Scientist will contribute to create the economic value at scale from both future growth and profitability perspectives.

In this role, you will be set on a constant “treasure hunt” for new data sources to apply the latest techniques for modeling projects. You will have unparalleled opportunity to apply state-of-the-art machine learning techniques to real world datasets. You will work on a team of PhD level statisticians to create ground-breaking predictive models using new analytical techniques that determine insurance pure premiums, customer life time value, customer buying behaviors and other related topics. This work will be used in setting pricing, underwriting, marketing, policy and claims service, fraud detection and product design strategies for the Plymouth Rock group of Companies. The role is not simply research: you will be part of a tactical team driving the profit and customer growth of an assigned business segment. The analysis and modeling will be tested via implementation both broadly and on a pilot basis to allow us to learn and refine our approaches in rapid succession.

By conducting innovative research through advanced analytics and technical sophistication, the Data Scientist will contribute to create the economic value at scale from both future growth and profitability perspectives.

In this role, you will be set on a constant “treasure hunt” for new data sources to apply the latest techniques for modeling projects. You will have unparalleled opportunity to apply state-of-the-art machine learning techniques to real world datasets. You will work on a team of PhD level statisticians to create ground-breaking predictive models using new analytical techniques that determine insurance pure premiums, customer life time value, customer buying behaviors and other related topics. This work will be used in setting pricing, underwriting, marketing, policy and claims service, fraud detection and product design strategies for the Plymouth Rock group of Companies. The role is not simply research: you will be part of a tactical team driving the profit and customer growth of an assigned business segment. The analysis and modeling will be tested via implementation both broadly and on a pilot basis to allow us to learn and refine our approaches in rapid succession.

This position offers excellent career growth and promotional opportunities, stellar compensation, and an opportunity to work with the world's premier provider of aftermarket automotive data. Hearst and MOTOR Information Systems could very well be your best and last position in a rewarding and fulfilling career!

Overview:
The Data Scientists will take a lead role in the end-to-end process of data science initiatives with limited supervision, including but not limited to model design, structure, development and post-deployment model performance monitoring. By conducting innovative research through advanced analytics and technical sophistication, the Data Scientist will contribute to create the economic value at scale from both future growth and profitability perspectives.

In this role, you will be set on a constant “treasure hunt” for new data sources to apply the latest techniques for modeling projects. You will have unparalleled opportunity to apply state-of-the-art machine learning techniques to real world datasets. You will work on a team of PhD level statisticians to create ground-breaking predictive models using new analytical techniques that determine insurance pure premiums, customer life time value, customer buying behaviors and other related topics. You will work on a team of PhD level statisticians to create ground-breaking predictive models using new analytical techniques that determine insurance pure premiums, customer life time value, customer buying behaviors and other related topics. This work will be used in setting pricing, underwriting, marketing, policy and claims service, fraud detection and product design strategies for the Plymouth Rock group of Companies. The role is not simply research: you will be part of a tactical team driving the profit and customer growth of an assigned business segment. The analysis and modeling will be tested via implementation both broadly and on a pilot basis to allow us to learn and refine our approaches in rapid succession.
